user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Only one image is provided (img_1), and while it shows the rear of the car, the alleged dent is not clearly visible or identifiable as a specific damage point for assessment.
claim_status_justification: The user claims there is a dent on the back of the car/rear bumper area. Image img_1 shows the rear of the vehicle, but the alleged dent is not clearly visible or identifiable in the provided image to confirm its existence or severity.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the front bumper area of the car where the scratch is claimed.
claim_status_justification: The images (img_1, img_2) clearly show a visible scratch mark on the front bumper area of the car, matching the user's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images clearly show the windshield and visible cracks consistent with the user's claim.
claim_status_justification: Both images (img_1 and img_2) clearly show the windshield with visible cracks, supporting the user's claim of damage from a small stone.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The claimed part (side mirror) is not clearly visible or inspectable for damage in the provided image.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the side mirror was damaged, but img_1 only shows a general view of the car's body and does not provide a clear, close-up inspection of the side mirror to confirm damage.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images provided (img_1 and img_2) do not clearly show the claimed damage to the rear bumper from a sufficient angle for proper assessment.
claim_status_justification: The user claims damage to the rear bumper after being tapped from behind. While img_1 and img_2 show the general area of the rear, the alleged dent or damage is not clearly visible or identifiable in either image due to poor angles and obstruction.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The claimed part (headlight) is not clearly visible or inspectable for cracks/damage in the provided image.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the headlight is cracked. However, in img_1, the headlight area is partially obscured and viewed at an angle that makes it impossible to definitively confirm a crack or damage.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the door panel area where the dent is claimed.
claim_status_justification: Image 1 and Image 2 both show a visible dent on the side door panel, supporting the user's claim.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows a scratch on the hood of the car.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows a visible scratch mark on the top panel of the car's hood, matching the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the laptop screen with visible cracks.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the display glass has a crack. Image img_1 clearly shows multiple visible cracks across the laptop screen, supporting the claim.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images provided (img_1 and img_2) do not clearly show the hinge area or any visible damage to the laptop's hinges as claimed. The focus is on the overall body/lid.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the hinge area is broken and the screen wobbles. While img_1 shows the laptop lid/body, and img_2 shows a side view of the body, neither image provides a clear, close-up view of the hinges or any visible breakage that supports the claim of mechanical failure.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images provided do not clearly show the keyboard area or any visible stains/damage related to water spillage as claimed.
claim_status_justification: The user claims a stain and sticky keys on the keyboard area due to water spillage. Image img_1 shows the laptop, but the specific area of the keyboard where the alleged stain or damage occurred is not clearly visible or identifiable in the provided image.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the corner of the laptop body with visible denting.
claim_status_justification: The user claims a dent on one corner of the laptop. Both img_1 and img_2 clearly show visible dents/dings on the outer corners of the laptop body.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The claimed damage is to the screen, but image img_1 does not provide a clear view of any visible cracks or shattering on the display area.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the screen looks shattered. Image img_1 shows the laptop open, but the display area is not clearly visible enough to confirm any cracks or shattering. The damage cannot be verified from the provided image.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image (img_1) shows the laptop trackpad area, but it is heavily obstructed and does not clearly show physical damage as claimed.
claim_status_justification: The user claims physical damage around the trackpad area. While img_1 shows the general area, the view is heavily cropped and obstructed by fingers/objects, making it impossible to definitively assess any physical damage (dent, scratch, crack) on the trackpad or surrounding body.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the corner of the package with visible crushing damage.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 clearly shows the corner of the box is crushed, matching the customer's claim.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the package exterior and the torn seal/tape area.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the packaging is torn or opened. Both img_1 and img_2 clearly show visible tears/rips in the tape and cardboard seal area of the package, supporting the claim of torn packaging.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows a stain on the package surface.
claim_status_justification: Image img_1 shows a visible wet-looking stain on the exterior surface of the package, which supports the customer's claim of water damage/stains.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images show an opened box (img_1) and some packaging materials/void fill (img_2), but they do not provide visual evidence of the missing item or contents area that would allow verification of a 'missing part' claim.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the item was missing from the box. While img_1 shows an opened package and img_2 shows internal packaging materials, neither image provides visual evidence of the specific product or contents area to confirm that an item is missing. The claim requires proof of absence, which cannot be visually verified here.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Image img_1 clearly shows the exterior of the package with visible crushing damage.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the outside box was badly crushed. Image img_1 clearly shows significant crushing damage to the exterior of the package, supporting the claim.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Both images clearly show the package exterior and the torn seal area.
claim_status_justification: The user claims the delivery box arrived opened and that the seal area looked torn. Both img_1 and img_2 clearly show visible tears/rips on the package's outer sealing tape/flap, supporting the claim of damaged packaging.
